nation: Chippewas of the Thames First Nation
one_time_description: Loss on impairment of assets held for sale - investment in Garden Variety cannabis retail company written down to recovery value
economic_notes: Big Bear Creek Trust assets of $115.3M (investments + cash). Green Lane Landfill revenue share agreement with City of Toronto at 40% of tonnage royalties. Solar farms operated through Development Corporation with Hydro One service contracts. Joint venture Aecon Three Fires (51% owned by Anishinaabeg Utilities Construction LP). OLG revenue stream present.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - Development Corporation (wholly-owned subsidiary) financial statements for FY2021, FY2022, and FY2023 were not available; Dev Corp figures remain frozen at March 31, 2020 balances and are unaudited. This is a multi-year qualification. Prior year figures restated due to understatement of Big Bear Creek community development revenue by $345,268. Big Bear Creek Trust ($115.3M in assets) and Land Claim Trust are administered but not consolidated. Two GBEs (A2 Utilities Construction Inc. and Anishinaabeg Utilities Construction LP) accounted for on modified equity basis at nominal $2. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $15,583,704 + Province of Ontario $4,533,367 + Health Canada $3,540,250 + Stepping Stones $2,922,280 + Southern First Nations Secretariat $2,757,877 + CMHC subsidy $260,835 + OFNLP $1,232,876 + UOI $1,219,374 - adjusted for deferred revenue netting makes direct calculation complex; figure provided is sum of identified government transfer lines only excluding Big Bear Creek and deferred revenue adjustments.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
